     The Numbers Business Report

The Last Voyage of the Demeter (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $13,637,180Details
International Box Office $6,760,468
Worldwide Box Office $20,397,648
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $858,178
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,669,937
Total $2,528,115
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The merchant ship Demeter is chartered to carry private cargo—fifty unmarked wooden crates—from Carpathia to London. Strange events befall the doomed crew as they attempt to survive the ocean voyage, stalked each night by a merciless presence onboard the ship. When the Demeter finally arrives off the shores of England, it is a charred, derelict wreck. There is no trace of the crew.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,504,950 (47.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.10 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$45,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,715 opening theaters/2,715 max. theaters, 2.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $14,908,654

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 11th, 2023 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: August 9th, 2023 (Wide), released as The Last Voyage of The Demeter (France)
August 10th, 2023 (Wide), released as The Last Voyage of The Demeter (Italy)
August 10th, 2023 (Wide), released as The Last Voyage of The Demeter (Brazil)
August 10th, 2023 (Wide), released as Dracula Voyage of the Demeter (Australia)
August 10th, 2023 (Wide), released as Dracula: Voyage of The Demeter (New Zealand)
Video Release: August 29th, 2023 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for bloody violence.
(Rating bulletin 2779 (cert #54397), 4/19/2023)
Running Time: 119 minutes
Filming location (country): Germany, Malta
Plot point: Doctors, On a Boat, Vampire
Subgenre: Gothic Horror
Time period setting: 1890s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Amblin Partners, DreamWorks Pictures, Universal Pictures, New Republic Pictures, Phoenix Pictures
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom, Germany, Italy, Malta
Languages: English

Strange events befall the doomed crew as they attempt to survive the ocean voyage, stalked each night by a merciless presence onboard the ship. When the Demeter finally arrives off the shores of England, it is a charred, derelict wreck. There is no trace of the crew.
